rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/26/2021. Continued again for same issue. 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) The proposed order must begin with Order For Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. 3) Petitioner must file notice being given on the mandatory notice of hearing form. 4) A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. Unless otherwise permitted for good cause, if an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three (3) years and each three- year period must be filed separately. [LRSF 14.35]. 5) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 6) Please explain why $651,685.83 is currently held in the Bank of America account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. 7) Please file the Seller's closing statement for the sale of the real property. 8) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.36 G.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN IRENE SODERLUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/21. (No earlier date available.) Paragraph XII still shows incorrect amounts for the appraised value and the loss on sale (although the correct amounts were shown on Schedule D of the account). The proposed distribution calculation in paragraph XXIV is incorrect. There were multiple costs advanced by the attorney for the Estate of Sherman Allen Soderlund but only $702 (not $705?) was returned on 6/26/20? Is there support for those costs NOT having been reimbursed to the attorney from that other estate? And why were E-filing costs increased from $5 to $11.50? (It would be helpful if costs showed the date of the payments.) The Request for Special Notice filed on 7/23/19 requested notice be sent to Probate Advance, LLC, not to any attorney. Notice must be served as specifically shown on a Request for Special Notice unless the requestor directs otherwise in writing (or waives notice). It appears that an amendment to the amended petition will need to be filed to address these issues (primarily because of the miscalculated proposed distribution). Courtesy copies of the amendment and proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/22/21. (Supplements or amendments (or amended petitions) should stand on their own and NOT refer to issues raised by the Court.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR GARAY ROSARIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/28/2021. 1) Paragraph 2 of the petition: The order determining the date of death has not been corrected. Probate Code sections 8005(b)(1)(A) & 8006(b). 2) Paragraph 11: The date of mailing notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be specified. LRSF 14.36(I)(2). 3) Paragraph 12: Has the petitioner taken any IAEA action reportable under CRC 7.250? 4) Paragraph 18: The valuation date is 12/29/2018 instead of 12/19/2018. 5) Paragraph 20: Are there outstanding bills due to any third party for preparing the account? LRSF 14.36(I)(6)(a)(iii). 6) Paragraph 28: When did Victor G. Rosario, Jr. move to the current address? 7) Paragraphs 29 & 30: A) Please provide legal authority for application of CCP sections 708.410 et seq. to a probate proceeding and beneficiary of a decedent's estate. B) No motion appears to have been filed under CCP section 708.470(a) for an order authorizing direct distribution to the judgment creditor. 8) Paragraph 33: The Court does not approve any single institution holding the estate funds exceeding the FDIC limit. A proof that the excess funds have been segregated and placed at a different institution must be filed. 9) Paragraph 34: The purpose of the reserve must be specified. LRSF 14.36(I)(3). 10) Schedule A: No interest payments between the date of death and 08/16/2019 are reported. 11) Schedule B: A) Please explain the nature of "fees" paid to the petitioner's attorney on 09/03/2019 & 10/10/2019. B) Please explain the reason for the reimbursement on 09/25/2019. 12) Schedule C: A) Was the estate the designated beneficiary of the life insurance? B) Please provide copies of receipts for the 4 items described as "Probate - $200". C) No creditor claim has been filed by the petitioner for the funeral expenses, nor has the claim been approved by the Court. Probate Code sections 9000(a)(3) & 9252. 13) No schedule of property on hand at close of account is on file. Probate Code section 1062(f). 14) No original bank statement verifying the ending balance is on file. LRSF 14.35(D) & 14.60(D). 15) The current bond is insufficient. An ex parte petition to increase the bond must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CATHERINE WHITCOMB TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Petition To Add Plaintiffs And Amend Petition
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody Petitioner Peter Whitcomb, Jr.'s Motion to Amend Petition to Add Plaintiffs and Amend Petition is GRANTED, notwithstanding Petitioner's failure to comply with the requirements of CRC, Rule 3.1324. Petitioner is ordered to file and serve the Amended Petition within 10 days of entry of this order. Petitioner shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on March 16, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MATTIE B. LYONS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: Determination That The Mattie B. Lyons Revocable Trust Is Invalid And Imposition Of Constructive Trust-Undue Influence.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/26/2021. Issues: Continued again for same issues no filed response. 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.]. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. 4) Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined before the underlining petition will be determined. The Petition on page 3 lines 12-15 alleges the principal place of administration is San Bernardino. Please provide the current trustee's address and legal authority for San Francisco being the proper venue. 5) Please give notice to Harold Lyons not just his attorney. (Probate Code 1202). 6) Please file a copy of the deed for 234 Minerva Street San Francisco. 7) Please file a copy of the Notification by Trustee. 8) Please explain page 2 line 28 and page 3 line 1 allege the petitioners will benefit by a juridical determination that the purported trust amendments are invalid. There appear to be no trust amendments just a trust attached to the petition please explain. 8) Please file legal authority in support of the causes of action.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of this hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner's Notes: a. The 12/08/2020 Supplement must be signed and verified by Petitioner. [CRC 7.103]. b. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not filed form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form [LRSF 14.18 E.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PHILIP M. EWING AND MARIAN S. EWING IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Transfer Trust To Another Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/26/2021. 1) A petition and account involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries, including all persons in being who may or will receive income or corpus of the trust. [CRC 7.902]. 2) Probate Code 17402 The petition for transfer shall set forth all of the following: (b) The names, ages, and places of residence of the living beneficiaries, as far as known to the petitioner. 3) Please explain and provide legal authority for why this petition is before the court. [Probate Code 17209]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/14/21. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES RICHARD OGDEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/14/2021. 1) No DE-121 form showing notice was given with a copy of the petition to the person who requested special notice is on file. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet (cannot be e-filed). 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) If the decedent was survived by his niece, item 6b of the petition must be checked. 6) The dates of death of all predeceased persons listed at item 8 must be identified. 7) Is Michael Ogden the decedent's only sibling? 8) Is Michelle Ogden the only child of Michael Ogden? 9) Did the petitioner have any personal or business relationship to the decedent? 10) Is the annual gross income alleged at item 3d(2)(b) an accurately estimate?
